name: DataFlow
summary: Core data flow library. Provides DataFlow::Node, DataFlow::ConfigSig, DataFlow::CallCfgNode and helpers for local/global flow.
usage_notes: All path queries must import this module. Mixes with TaintTracking for end-to-end flows. Works with nodes from control/data flow graph. Function parameters appear as DataFlow::ParameterNode (avoid Function::Parameter.* APIs).

name: TaintTracking
summary: High-level taint tracking interface built on DataFlow. Supports global taint configs via TaintTracking::Global<Config>.
usage_notes: Instantiate once per query: module Flow = TaintTracking::Global<Config>. Add import Flow::PathGraph to access Flow::PathNode and flowPath.

name: RemoteFlowSources
summary: Models remote/user-controlled input sources. RemoteFlowSource covers frameworks like Django, Flask, FastAPI.
usage_notes: Mark sources with `src instanceof RemoteFlowSource`. Extend RemoteFlowSource::Range in custom modelling packs if needed.

name: BarrierGuards
summary: Predefined guard nodes that block taint flows when certain comparisons succeed.
usage_notes: Use inside sanitizers or additional flow logic to recognise equality checks against constants.

name: FlowSummary
summary: Helpers for modelling library call summaries using SummarizedCallable.
usage_notes: Extend SummarizedCallable when built-in models miss a call. Combine with Config::isAdditionalFlowStep to hook into taint.

name: Regexp
summary: Represents data that is used as part of regular expressions.
usage_notes: Use when tracking strings that become regex patterns, e.g., to detect ReDoS or unsafe regex building.

name: SensitiveDataSources
summary: Models sensitive data such as passwords and API keys.
usage_notes: Use as high-signal sources when queries focus on secrets exfiltration.

name: TypeTracking
summary: Tracks object capabilities or inferred types via TypeTracker.
usage_notes: Combine with DataFlow nodes to correlate objects with methods they provide, e.g., to identify HTTP clients.
